title: potentilla frutescens
intro: Potentilla frutescens, also known as prickly cinquefoil or shrubby cinquefoil, is a flowering shrub native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Potentilla frutescens have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, minor wounds, and digestive issues.
